summary:
Claim: The most probable diagnosis is Stevens-Johnson Syndrome (SJS).  
Evidence: - Generalized skin peeling excluding palms and soles is characteristic of SJS; this pattern is highly suggestive. - Symptoms develop within days to weeks after initiating anti-epileptic drugs—consistent with medication-induced cutaneous reactions typical of SJS.  
Answer: C
